story: The day Milly saw the hit-and-run, she recognized the boy at the wheel—Parker Sumter, her ninth-grade student. She phoned police and said she hadn’t seen the driver. Mr. Bradshaw died. Years passed; Parker graduated. After six years, Parker has kept clippings in a drawer and, today, intends to walk into the county station after work and confess. After her last class, the sky is blank and gray. Milly stands by her car outside the school.
implausible_knowledge: After six years, Parker has kept clippings in a drawer and, today, intends to walk into the county station after work and confess.
question: After school, what will she most likely do about the hit-and-run?
answer_with_knowledge: Drive to the county station to offer a statement.
